steps.title: Build the causal claim
steps.content: The notes connect reduced hunting time to understimulation and then to more vessel interactions. They also describe those interactions as damaging vessels by ramming, nudging, or biting rudders.

steps.title: Match the claim to evidence
steps.content: B reverses the notes by claiming orca behavior caused tuna increases. C overstates TAST by calling it effective before further testing. D reports the workshop and incidents but does not explain the behavior. The best choice links destructive behavior to understimulation caused by less hunting. The answer is A.
